marketState: bearish — extreme near-term heat masking medium-term oversupply and negative seasonality
summary: Front price of $3.28 sits 11% above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 but remains inside the model band ($2.46–$3.46), with the premium driven by 1–7 day weather demand that has breached the 5-year maximum — the most extreme reading in the dataset. That heat advantage reverses sharply in the 8–16 day window, which falls to only the 15th percentile vs 5 years, and the 24-hour demand trend is already declining (-3.2 Bcf/d). Storage is a structural headwind at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a mildly bearish +6 Bcf weekly surprise, and seasonal probability assigns just a 19% chance of higher prices four weeks out with a typical drift of -1.8%. The one meaningful counterweight is COT: managed money net short is at the 18th percentile historically, a contrarian-bullish extreme that sets a floor and creates short-squeeze risk if any weather surprise materialises.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal probability is the dominant signal: history shows only a 19% chance of higher prices in four weeks with a typical 4-week move of -1.8%, and while reliability is low (wide spread), the directional lean must be respected. Storage surplus of +151 Bcf above the 5-year average and a bearish +6 Bcf weekly surprise provide a structural ceiling on any sustained rally, as re-fill economics remain unfavourable for bulls. The fair-value spread converges from +$0.32 now to +$0.18 at +21 days, confirming the current premium is weather-transient; COT at the 18th percentile limits conviction and demands a wide stop above the fair-value band ($3.46 upper bound).
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained daily close above $3.55 (beyond the current fair-value upper band of $3.46); weekly EIA storage builds consistently below 50 Bcf narrowing the surplus toward flat; or 8–16 day weather demand jumping back above the 5-year median.

shortTerm.rationale: The 5m RSI at 30.5 (oversold) combined with 1–7 day demand that has breached the 5-year maximum creates an unfavourable near-term short entry; price could bounce 7–12 cents before the weather premium fades. Selling into current conditions means fighting both the oversold intraday condition and the most extreme demand print in the 5-year range, compressing the risk-reward. Waiting for a bounce to a defined resistance zone then entering short as the 8–16 day demand normalisation (15th percentile vs 5y) becomes the market narrative offers a materially better entry.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for 5m oversold recovery bounce toward $3.35–$3.40 (weather premium exhaustion zone, approaching the +7D fair-value upper band of $3.445); enter SHORT on a 1h momentum reversal (RSI crossing back below 50 or bearish MACD crossover) once the 8–16D demand outlook at the 15th percentile is confirmed. Alternatively, if no bounce materialises and price breaks below $3.22 on volume, enter short directly as bear momentum accelerates through the fair-value midpoint.

8–16D demand collapses to 15th percentile vs 5y, removing weather support and exposing the bear thesis within the trade window
Storage surplus +151 Bcf above 5-year average with a bearish +6 Bcf weekly surprise — structural headwind capping sustained rallies
Seasonality: 81% historical probability of lower price in 4 weeks, typical 4-week drift -1.8%; direction clear despite low reliability
COT managed-money net short at 18th percentile (5y) — historically extreme bearish positioning acting as a contrarian floor and short-squeeze risk
risks: Extended or intensifying heat dome beyond the 7-day forecast keeps demand above 5-year max, potentially triggering a short squeeze on the record speculator short book
Winter LNG export expansion or early cold-season expectations accelerate Nov–Jan strip pricing (Jan 2027 already at $4.437), dragging the front month higher via roll dynamics
